---
title: "4. Parameter exploration and inference"
subtitle: "Sessions 4.1 · 4.2"
toc: false
---

In this chapter you will fit personalized whole-brain models to empirical data
using TVB-Optim's gradient-based optimisation. Two complementary approaches
are covered: a disease use case with biomedical data integration, and a
systematic comparison of homogeneous versus heterogeneous parameter strategies.

## Sessions

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### [4a · Alzheimer's disease use case](04a_alzheimer_usecase.qmd)
Build a personalised model using patient-specific connectivity and tau
deposition maps. Reproduce empirical BOLD signals and interpret the result in
the context of network degeneration.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### [4b · Parameter fitting](04b_parameter_fitting.qmd)
Compare homogeneous and heterogeneous parameter fitting strategies.
Use TVB-Optim to optimise a frequency gradient (or another map from TVBase)
and assess the impact on model fit quality.
:::

:::

---

*Part of [Day 1](day1.qmd)*
